systems.name: lazysite
systems.tagline: A file-based engine - drop in Markdown, no database, with optional paid pages.
systems.best_for: Owners who want their content as plain files, low maintenance and low cost, optional pay-per-read, and the freedom to let an AI manage it.

systems.name: Ghost
systems.tagline: A modern publishing platform built around posts, paid memberships and email newsletters.
systems.best_for: Professional writers and publishers who want memberships, subscriptions and newsletters out of the box and a polished editor, and are happy to run or pay for a Node and database stack.

rows.label: Footprint
rows.lazysite: One CGI script plus Perl modules
rows.ghost: A Node.js application with a database

rows.label: Database
rows.ghost: Required (MySQL, or SQLite for small installs)

rows.label: Focus
rows.lazysite: General sites, docs and content, with optional paid pages
rows.ghost: Professional publishing: posts, newsletters and memberships

rows.label: Memberships & paid content
rows.lazysite: Pay-per-read (x402) and per-user access, built in
rows.ghost: Built-in memberships and subscriptions via Stripe

rows.label: Email newsletters
rows.ghost: Built in - send posts as newsletters

rows.label: AI-first management
rows.lazysite: An agent publishes over WebDAV, a control API or MCP
rows.ghost: A content API exists, but the product centres on its editor

rows.label: Hosting
rows.lazysite: Any CGI host, or its own built-in server; very cheap
rows.ghost: A Node host you manage, or paid Ghost(Pro)

rows.label: Themes
rows.lazysite: Layout plus theme tokens; one file to re-skin

rows.label: Maintenance
rows.lazysite: Static files, no database, a small codebase
rows.ghost: Node runtime, a database and updates to keep current
